page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.12xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server optimized for high-performance computing workloads. It features 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 32.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong memory and computational capabilities. Memory bandwidth performance is particularly notable, with uncached large-block read speeds reaching 635.15 GB/sec and write speeds reaching 377.27 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with Geekbench and Passmark CPU evaluations, consistently rank in the top 25%. LLM inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds of 2622.16 tokens/sec for small models and strong text generation speeds of 36.8 tokens/sec for medium models.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers high memory capacity and memory bandwidth per core, making it highly efficient for memory-bound parallel processing. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage operations must rely on network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps, which facilitates rapid data transfer to compensate for the lack of local disks.

This hardware configuration is designed for memory-intensive computational workloads, large-scale simulations, molecular modeling, finite element analysis, and parallelized scientific computing. It is also configured for memory-bound database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference tasks that benefit from AVX-512 vector instructions.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.12xlarge is an x86_64 server designed for high-performance computing, featuring 24 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a high allocation of 32.0 GB per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying instead on a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds of 635.15 GB/sec and write speeds of 377.27 GB/sec placing in the top 10%. CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests consistently ranks in the top 25%. This server is suited for memory-bound parallel processing, scientific simulations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.12xlarge is an x86_64 HPC server featuring 24 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, 150 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.12xlarge features 24 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 150 Gbps networking for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute node featuring massive DDR5 memory bandwidth and high-speed networking for parallel scientific workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 32 GB per core
Top-tier uncached memory read bandwidth of 635.15 GB/sec
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
AVX-512 and vector neural network instructions enabled
Suited for scientific simulations, molecular modeling, and CPU-based inference
